title: Tender Roasted Canadian Pork Skillet
description: A hearty Canadian dish featuring roasted pork, pineapple juice, and a blend of spices, perfect for a chilly evening. This low-fat-friendly recipe is easy to make and serves a crowd.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat.
Sear the pork tenderloin until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the pork from the skillet and set aside.
Add the chopped onion to the skillet and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for 1 minute.
Add the pineapple juice, thyme, salt, and pepper to the skillet, stirring to combine.
Return the pork tenderloin to the skillet and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and roast for 20 minutes.
Add the cubed potatoes to the skillet and continue to roast for an additional 20 minutes, or until the pork is cooked through and the potatoes are tender.
